I found the repo split across `core`, `api`, `backend`, `admin`, `promotions`, `legacy_promotions`, and sample/demo gems. I’m now narrowing on the `Spree::Order` model, its concerns/state machine, and the service/controller layers that mutate or recalculate it.

content.text:
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  # The customers cart until completed, then acts as permanent record of the transaction.
     5	  #
     6	  # `Spree::Order` is the heart of the Solidus system, as it acts as the customer's
     7	  # cart as they shop. Once an order is complete, it serves as the
     8	  # permanent record of their purchase. It has many responsibilities:
     9	  #
    10	  # * Records and validates attributes like `total` and relationships like
    11	  # `Spree::LineItem` as an ActiveRecord model.
    12	  #
    13	  # * Implements a customizable state machine to manage the lifecycle of an order.
    14	  #
    15	  # * Implements business logic to provide a single interface for quesitons like
    16	  # `checkout_allowed?` or `payment_required?`.
    17	  #
    18	  #  * Implements an interface for mutating the order with methods like
    19	  # `empty!` and `fulfill!`.
    20	  #
    21	  class Order < Spree::Base
    22	    ORDER_NUMBER_LENGTH = 9
    23	    ORDER_NUMBER_LETTERS = false
    24	    ORDER_NUMBER_PREFIX = "R"
    25	
    26	    include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.order
    27	
    28	    include Spree::Order::Payments
    29	    include Metadata
    30	
    31	    class InsufficientStock < StandardError
    32	      attr_reader :items
    33	
    34	      def initialize(message = nil, items: {})
    35	        @items = items
    36	        super(message)
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    class CannotRebuildShipments < StandardError; end
    41	
    42	    extend Spree::DisplayMoney
    43	
    44	    money_methods(

   472	        code.strip.downcase
   473	      rescue
   474	        nil
   475	      end
   476	    end
   477	
   478	    def can_add_coupon?
   479	      Spree::Config.promotions.coupon_code_handler_class.new(self).can_apply?
   480	    end
   481	
   482	    def shipped?
   483	      %w[partial shipped].include?(shipment_state)
   484	    end
   485	
   486	    def ensure_shipping_address
   487	      unless ship_address&.valid?
   488	        errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.ship_address_required")) && (return false)
   489	      end
   490	    end
   491	
   492	    def ensure_billing_address
   493	      return unless billing_address_required?
   494	      return if bill_address&.valid?
   495	
   496	      errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.bill_address_required"))
   497	      false
   498	    end
   499	
   500	    def billing_address_required?
   501	      Spree::Config.billing_address_required
   502	    end
   503	
   504	    def create_proposed_shipments
   505	      if completed?
   506	        raise CannotRebuildShipments.new(I18n.t("spree.cannot_rebuild_shipments_order_completed"))
   507	      elsif shipments.any? { |shipment| !shipment.pending? }
   508	        raise CannotRebuildShipments.new(I18n.t("spree.cannot_rebuild_shipments_shipments_not_pending"))
   509	      else
   510	        shipments.destroy_all
   511	        shipments.push(*Spree::Config.stock.coordinator_class.new(self).shipments)
   512	      end
   513	    end
   514	
   515	    def create_shipments_for_line_item(line_item)
   516	      inventory_units = Spree::Config.stock.inventory_unit_builder_class.new(self).missing_units_for_line_item(line_item)
   517	
   518	      Spree::Config.stock.coordinator_class.new(self, inventory_units:).shipments.each do |shipment|
   519	        shipments << shipment
   520	      end
   521	    end
   522	
   523	    # Clean shipments and make order back to address state (or to whatever state
   524	    # is set by restart_checkout_flow in case of state machine modifications)
   525	    def check_shipments_and_restart_checkout
   526	      if !completed? && shipments.all?(&:pending?)
   527	        shipments.destroy_all
   528	        update_column(:shipment_total, 0)
   529	        restart_checkout_flow
   530	      end
   531	    end
   532	
   533	    alias_method :ensure_updated_shipments, :check_shipments_and_restart_checkout
   534	    deprecate ensure_updated_shipments: :check_shipments_and_restart_checkout, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   535	
   536	    def restart_checkout_flow
   537	      return if state == "cart"
   538	
   539	      update_columns(
   540	        state: "cart",
   541	        updated_at: Time.current
   542	      )
   543	      self.next if line_items.any?
   544	    end
   545	
   546	    def refresh_shipment_rates
   547	      shipments.map(&:refresh_rates)
   548	    end
   549	
   550	    def shipping_eq_billing_address?
   551	      bill_address == ship_address
   552	    end
   553	
   554	    def is_risky?
   555	      payments.risky.count > 0
   556	    end
   557	
   558	    def canceled_by(user)
   559	      transaction do
   560	        cancel!
   561	        update_column(:canceler_id, user.id)
   562	      end
   563	    end
   564	
   565	    def approved?
   566	      !!approved_at
   567	    end
   568	
   569	    def can_approve?
   570	      !approved?
   571	    end
   572	
   573	    def quantity
   574	      line_items.sum(:quantity)
   575	    end
   576	
   577	    def has_non_reimbursement_related_refunds?
   578	      refunds.non_reimbursement.exists?
   579	    end
   580	
   581	    def tax_total
   582	      additional_tax_total + included_tax_total
   583	    end
   584	
   585	    def add_store_credit_payments
   586	      return if user.nil?
   587	      return if payments.store_credits.checkout.empty? && user.available_store_credit_total(currency:).zero?
   588	
   589	      payments.store_credits.checkout.each(&:invalidate!)
   590	
   591	      # this can happen when multiple payments are present, auto_capture is
   592	      # turned off, and one of the payments fails when the user tries to
   593	      # complete the order, which sends the order back to the 'payment' state.
   594	      authorized_total = payments.pending.sum(:amount)
   595	
   596	      remaining_total = outstanding_balance - authorized_total
   597	
   598	      matching_store_credits = user.store_credits.where(currency:)
   599	
   600	      if matching_store_credits.any?
   601	        payment_method = Spree::PaymentMethod::StoreCredit.first
   602	        sorter = Spree::Config.store_credit_prioritizer_class.new(matching_store_credits, self)
   603	
   604	        sorter.call.each do |credit|
   605	          break if remaining_total.zero?
   606	          next if credit.amount_remaining.zero?
   607	
   608	          amount_to_take = [credit.amount_remaining, remaining_total].min
   609	          payments.create!(source: credit,
   610	            payment_method:,
   611	            amount: amount_to_take,
   612	            state: "checkout",
   613	            response_code: credit.generate_authorization_code)
   614	          remaining_total -= amount_to_take
   615	        end
   616	      end
   617	
   618	      other_payments = payments.checkout.not_store_credits
   619	      if remaining_total.zero?
   620	        other_payments.each(&:invalidate!)

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  class Order < Spree::Base
     5	    module Payments
     6	      # processes any pending payments and must return a boolean as it's
     7	      # return value is used by the checkout state_machine to determine
     8	      # success or failure of the 'complete' event for the order
     9	      #
    10	      # Returns:
    11	      #
    12	      # - true if all pending payments processed successfully
    13	      #
    14	      # - true if a payment failed, ie. raised a GatewayError
    15	      #   which gets rescued and converted to TRUE when
    16	      #   :allow_checkout_gateway_error is set to true
    17	      #
    18	      # - false if a payment failed, ie. raised a GatewayError
    19	      #   which gets rescued and converted to FALSE when
    20	      #   :allow_checkout_on_gateway_error is set to false
    21	      #
    22	      def process_payments!
    23	        process_payments_with(:process!)
    24	      end
    25	
    26	      def authorize_payments!
    27	        process_payments_with(:authorize!)
    28	      end
    29	
    30	      def capture_payments!
    31	        process_payments_with(:purchase!)
    32	      end
    33	
    34	      def unprocessed_payments
    35	        payments.select(&:checkout?)
    36	      end
    37	
    38	      private
    39	
    40	      def process_payments_with(method_name)
    41	        # Don't run if there is nothing to pay.
    42	        return true if payment_total >= total
    43	
    44	        unprocessed_payments.each do |payment|
    45	          break if payment_total >= total
    46	
    47	          payment.public_send(method_name)
    48	        end
    49	      rescue Core::GatewayError => error
    50	        result = !!Spree::Config[:allow_checkout_on_gateway_error]
    51	        errors.add(:base, error.message) && (return result)
    52	      end
    53	    end
    54	  end
    55	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  module Core
     5	    class StateMachines
     6	      module Order
     7	        module ClassMethods
     8	          attr_accessor :previous_states
     9	          attr_writer :next_event_transitions, :checkout_steps, :removed_transitions
    10	
    11	          def checkout_flow(&block)
    12	            if block_given?
    13	              @checkout_flow = block
    14	              define_state_machine!
    15	            else
    16	              @checkout_flow
    17	            end
    18	          end
    19	
    20	          def define_state_machine!
    21	            self.checkout_steps = {}
    22	            self.next_event_transitions = []
    23	            self.previous_states = [:cart]
    24	            self.removed_transitions = []
    25	
    26	            # Build the checkout flow using the checkout_flow defined either
    27	            # within the Order class, or a decorator for that class.
    28	            #
    29	            # This method may be called multiple times depending on if the
    30	            # checkout_flow is re-defined in a decorator or not.
    31	            instance_eval(&checkout_flow)
    32	
    33	            klass = self
    34	
    35	            # To avoid multiple occurrences of the same transition being defined
    36	            # On first definition, state_machines will not be defined
    37	            state_machines.clear if respond_to?(:state_machines)
    38	            state_machine :state, initial: :cart, use_transactions: false do
    39	              klass.next_event_transitions.each { |state| transition(state.merge(on: :next)) }
    40	
    41	              event :cancel do
    42	                transition to: :canceled, if: :allow_cancel?, from: :complete
    43	              end
    44	
    45	              event :return do
    46	                transition to: :returned, from: [:complete, :awaiting_return], if: :all_inventory_units_returned?
    47	              end
    48	
    49	              event :resume do
    50	                transition to: :resumed, from: :canceled, if: :canceled?
    51	              end
    52	
    53	              event :authorize_return do
    54	                transition to: :awaiting_return, from: :complete
    55	              end
    56	
    57	              event :complete do
    58	                transition to: :complete, from: klass.checkout_steps.keys.last
    59	              end
    60	
    61	              if states[:payment]
    62	                event :payment_failed do
    63	                  transition to: :payment, from: :confirm
    64	                end
    65	
    66	                after_transition to: :complete, do: :add_payment_sources_to_wallet
    67	                before_transition to: :payment, do: :add_default_payment_from_wallet
    68	                before_transition to: :payment, do: :ensure_billing_address
    69	
    70	                before_transition to: :confirm, do: :add_store_credit_payments
    71	
    72	                # see also process_payments_before_complete below which needs to
    73	                # be added in the correct sequence.
    74	              end
    75	
    76	              before_transition from: :cart, do: :ensure_line_items_present
    77	
    78	              if states[:address]
    79	                before_transition to: :address, do: :assign_default_user_addresses
    80	                before_transition from: :address, do: :persist_user_address!
    81	              end
    82	
    83	              if states[:delivery]
    84	                before_transition to: :delivery, do: :ensure_shipping_address
    85	                before_transition to: :delivery, do: :create_proposed_shipments
    86	                before_transition to: :delivery, do: :ensure_available_shipping_rates
    87	              end
    88	
    89	              before_transition to: :resumed, do: :ensure_line_item_variants_are_not_deleted
    90	              before_transition to: :resumed, do: :validate_line_item_availability
    91	
    92	              # Sequence of before_transition to: :complete
    93	              # calls matter so that we do not process payments
    94	              # until validations have passed
    95	              before_transition to: :complete, do: :validate_line_item_availability
    96	              before_transition to: :complete, do: :ensure_promotions_eligible
    97	              before_transition to: :complete, do: :ensure_line_item_variants_are_not_deleted
    98	              before_transition to: :complete, do: :ensure_inventory_units
    99	              if states[:payment]
   100	                before_transition to: :complete, do: :process_payments_before_complete
   101	              end
   102	
   103	              after_transition to: :complete, do: :finalize
   104	              after_transition to: :resumed, do: :after_resume
   105	              after_transition to: :canceled, do: :after_cancel
   106	
   107	              after_transition from: any - :cart, to: any - [:confirm, :complete] do |order|
   108	                order.recalculate
   109	              end
   110	
   111	              after_transition do |order, transition|
   112	                order.logger.debug "Order #{order.number} transitioned from #{transition.from} to #{transition.to} via #{transition.event}"
   113	              end
   114	
   115	              after_failure do |order, transition|
   116	                order.logger.debug "Order #{order.number} halted transition on event #{transition.event} state #{transition.from}: #{order.errors.full_messages.join}"
   117	              end
   118	            end
   119	          end
   120	
   121	          def go_to_state(name, options = {})
   122	            checkout_steps[name] = options
   123	            previous_states.each do |state|
   124	              add_transition({from: state, to: name}.merge(options))
   125	            end
   126	            if options[:if]
   127	              previous_states << name
   128	            else
   129	              self.previous_states = [name]
   130	            end
   131	          end
   132	
   133	          def insert_checkout_step(name, options = {})
   134	            before = options.delete(:before)
   135	            after = options.delete(:after) unless before
   136	            after = checkout_steps.keys.last unless before || after
   137	
   138	            cloned_steps = checkout_steps.clone
   139	            cloned_removed_transitions = removed_transitions.clone
   140	            checkout_flow do
   141	              cloned_steps.each_pair do |key, value|
   142	                go_to_state(name, options) if key == before
   143	                go_to_state(key, value)
   144	                go_to_state(name, options) if key == after
   145	              end
   146	              cloned_removed_transitions.each do |transition|
   147	                remove_transition(transition)
   148	              end
   149	            end
   150	          end
   151	
   152	          def remove_checkout_step(name)
   153	            cloned_steps = checkout_steps.clone
   154	            cloned_removed_transitions = removed_transitions.clone
   155	            checkout_flow do
   156	              cloned_steps.each_pair do |key, value|
   157	                go_to_state(key, value) unless key == name
   158	              end
   159	              cloned_removed_transitions.each do |transition|
   160	                remove_transition(transition)
   161	              end
   162	            end
   163	          end
   164	
   165	          def remove_transition(options = {})
   166	            removed_transitions << options
   167	            next_event_transitions.delete(find_transition(options))
   168	          end
   169	
   170	          def find_transition(options = {})
   171	            return nil if options.nil? || !options.include?(:from) || !options.include?(:to)
   172	
   173	            next_event_transitions.detect do |transition|
   174	              transition[options[:from].to_sym] == options[:to].to_sym
   175	            end
   176	          end
   177	
   178	          def next_event_transitions
   179	            @next_event_transitions ||= []
   180	          end
   181	
   182	          def checkout_steps
   183	            @checkout_steps ||= {}
   184	          end
   185	
   186	          def checkout_step_names
   187	            checkout_steps.keys
   188	          end
   189	
   190	          def add_transition(options)
   191	            next_event_transitions << {options.delete(:from) => options.delete(:to)}.merge(options)
   192	          end
   193	
   194	          def removed_transitions
   195	            @removed_transitions ||= []
   196	          end
   197	        end
   198	      end
   199	    end
   200	  end
   201	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  # Generates order numbers
     5	  #
     6	  # In order to change the way your order numbers get generated you can either
     7	  # set your own instance of this class in your stores configuration with different options:
     8	  #
     9	  #     Spree::Config.order_number_generator = Spree::Order::NumberGenerator.new(
    10	  #       prefix: 'B',
    11	  #       lenght: 8,
    12	  #       letters: false
    13	  #     )
    14	  #
    15	  # or create your own class:
    16	  #
    17	  #     Spree::Config.order_number_generator = My::OrderNumberGenerator.new
    18	  #
    19	  class Order::NumberGenerator
    20	    attr_reader :letters, :prefix
    21	
    22	    def initialize(options = {})
    23	      @length = options[:length] || Spree::Order::ORDER_NUMBER_LENGTH
    24	      @letters = options[:letters] || Spree::Order::ORDER_NUMBER_LETTERS
    25	      @prefix = options[:prefix] || Spree::Order::ORDER_NUMBER_PREFIX
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    def generate
    29	      possible = (0..9).to_a
    30	      possible += ("A".."Z").to_a if letters
    31	
    32	      loop do
    33	        # Make a random number.
    34	        random = "#{prefix}#{(0...@length).map { possible.sample }.join}"
    35	        # Use the random number if no other order exists with it.
    36	        if Spree::Order.exists?(number: random)
    37	          # If over half of all possible options are taken add another digit.
    38	          @length += 1 if order_count > (10**@length / 2)
    39	        else
    40	          break random
    41	        end
    42	      end
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    private
    46	
    47	    def order_count
    48	      @order_count ||= Spree::Order.count
    49	    end
    50	  end
    51	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	# A service layer that handles generating Carton objects when inventory units
     4	# are actually shipped.  It also takes care of things like updating order and
     5	# shipment states and delivering shipment emails as needed.
     6	class Spree::OrderShipping
     7	  def initialize(order)
     8	    @order = order
     9	  end
    10	
    11	  # A shortcut method that ships *all* inventory units in a shipment in a single
    12	  # carton.  See also {#ship}.
    13	  #
    14	  # @param shipment The shipment to create a carton from.
    15	  # @param external_number An optional external number. e.g. from a shipping company or 3PL.
    16	  # @param tracking_number An optional tracking number.
    17	  # @return The carton created.
    18	  def ship_shipment(shipment, external_number: nil, tracking_number: nil, suppress_mailer: false)
    19	    ship(
    20	      inventory_units: shipment.inventory_units.shippable,
    21	      stock_location: shipment.stock_location,
    22	      address: shipment.order.ship_address,
    23	      shipping_method: shipment.shipping_method,
    24	      shipped_at: Time.current,
    25	      external_number:,
    26	      # TODO: Remove the `|| shipment.tracking` once Shipment#ship! is called by
    27	      # OrderShipping#ship rather than vice versa
    28	      tracking_number: tracking_number || shipment.tracking,
    29	      suppress_mailer:
    30	    )
    31	  end
    32	
    33	  # Generate a carton from the supplied inventory units and marks those units
    34	  # as shipped.  Also sends shipment emails if appropriate and updates
    35	  # shipment_states for associated orders.
    36	  #
    37	  # @param inventory_units The units to put in a carton together.
    38	  # @param stock_location The location the carton shipped from.
    39	  # @param address The address the carton was shipped to.
    40	  # @param shipping_method Shipping method used for the carton.
    41	  # @param shipped_at The time at which the shipment was shipped.
    42	  # @param external_number An optional external number. e.g. from a shipping company or 3PL.
    43	  # @param tracking_number An option tracking number.
    44	  # @return The carton created.
    45	  def ship(inventory_units:, stock_location:, address:, shipping_method:,
    46	    shipped_at: Time.current, external_number: nil, tracking_number: nil, suppress_mailer: false)
    47	    carton = nil
    48	
    49	    Spree::InventoryUnit.transaction do
    50	      inventory_units.each(&:ship!)
    51	
    52	      carton = Spree::Carton.create!(
    53	        stock_location:,
    54	        address:,
    55	        shipping_method:,
    56	        inventory_units:,
    57	        shipped_at:,
    58	        external_number:,
    59	        suppress_email: suppress_mailer,
    60	        tracking: tracking_number
    61	      )
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    inventory_units.map(&:shipment).uniq.each do |shipment|
    65	      if shipment.inventory_units.reload.all? { |iu| iu.shipped? || iu.canceled? }
    66	        shipment.update!(state: "shipped", shipped_at: Time.current, tracking: tracking_number)
    67	      else
    68	        shipment.update!(tracking: tracking_number)
    69	      end
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    @order.shipments.reload
    73	    @order.recalculate
    74	
    75	    Spree::Bus.publish(:carton_shipped, carton:)
    76	    carton
    77	  end
    78	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  class OrderInventory
     5	    attr_accessor :order, :line_item, :variant
     6	
     7	    def initialize(order, line_item)
     8	      @order = order
     9	      @line_item = line_item
    10	      @variant = line_item.variant
    11	    end
    12	
    13	    # Only verify inventory for completed orders (as orders in frontend checkout
    14	    # have inventory assigned via +order.create_proposed_shipment+) or when
    15	    # shipment is explicitly passed
    16	    #
    17	    # In case shipment is passed the stock location should only unstock or
    18	    # restock items if the order is completed. That is so because stock items
    19	    # are always unstocked when the order is completed through +shipment.finalize+
    20	    def verify(shipment = nil)
    21	      if order.completed? || shipment.present?
    22	
    23	        existing_quantity = inventory_units.count
    24	        desired_quantity = line_item.quantity - existing_quantity
    25	        if desired_quantity > 0
    26	          shipment ||= determine_target_shipment(desired_quantity)
    27	          if shipment
    28	            add_to_shipment(shipment, desired_quantity)
    29	          else
    30	            order.create_shipments_for_line_item(line_item).each do |new_shipment|
    31	              new_shipment.finalize!
    32	            end
    33	          end
    34	        elsif desired_quantity < 0
    35	          remove(-desired_quantity, shipment)
    36	        end
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    def inventory_units
    41	      line_item.inventory_units
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    private
    45	
    46	    def remove(quantity, shipment = nil)
    47	      if shipment.present?
    48	        remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
    49	      else
    50	        remove_from_any_shipment(quantity)
    51	      end
    52	    end
    53	
    54	    # Returns either one of the shipment:
    55	    #
    56	    # first unshipped that already includes this variant
    57	    # first unshipped that's leaving from a stock_location that stocks this variant, with availability check
    58	    # first unshipped that's leaving from a stock_location that stocks this variant, without availability check
    59	    def determine_target_shipment(quantity)
    60	      potential_shipments = order.shipments.order(:created_at, :id).select(&:ready_or_pending?)
    61	
    62	      potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
    63	        shipment.include?(variant)
    64	      end || begin
    65	        potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
    66	          Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant, shipment.stock_location).can_supply?(quantity)
    67	        end
    68	      end || potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
    69	        variant.stock_location_ids.include?(shipment.stock_location_id)
    70	      end
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    def add_to_shipment(shipment, quantity)
    74	      pending_units = []
    75	      if variant.should_track_inventory?
    76	        on_hand, back_order = shipment.stock_location.fill_status(variant, quantity)
    77	
    78	        on_hand.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("on_hand", variant, order, line_item) }
    79	        back_order.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("backordered", variant, order, line_item) }
    80	      else
    81	        quantity.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("on_hand", variant, order, line_item) }
    82	      end
    83	
    84	      # adding to this shipment, and removing from stock_location
    85	      if order.completed?
    86	        Spree::Stock::InventoryUnitsFinalizer.new(pending_units).run!
    87	      end
    88	
    89	      quantity
    90	    end
    91	
    92	    def remove_from_any_shipment(quantity)
    93	      order.shipments.each do |shipment|
    94	        break if quantity == 0
    95	        quantity -= remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
    96	      end
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    def remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
   100	      return 0 if quantity == 0 || shipment.shipped?
   101	
   102	      shipment_units = shipment.inventory_units_for_item(line_item, variant).reject do |variant_unit|
   103	        # TODO: exclude all 'shipped' states
   104	        variant_unit.state == "shipped"
   105	      end.sort_by(&:state)
   106	
   107	      removed_quantity = 0
   108	
   109	      shipment_units.each do |inventory_unit|
   110	        break if removed_quantity == quantity
   111	        inventory_unit.destroy
   112	        removed_quantity += 1
   113	      end
   114	
   115	      if shipment.inventory_units.count.zero?
   116	        order.shipments.destroy(shipment)
   117	      end
   118	
   119	      # removing this from shipment, and adding to stock_location
   120	      if order.completed?
   121	        shipment.stock_location.restock variant, removed_quantity, shipment
   122	      end
   123	
   124	      removed_quantity
   125	    end
   126	  end
   127	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  # Spree::OrderMerger is responsible for taking two orders and merging them
     5	  # together by adding the line items from additional orders to the order
     6	  # that the OrderMerger is initialized with.
     7	  #
     8	  # Orders that are merged should be incomplete orders which should belong to
     9	  # the same user. They should also be in the same currency.
    10	  class OrderMerger
    11	    # @!attribute order
    12	    #   @api public
    13	    #   @return [Spree::Order] The order which items wll be merged into.
    14	    attr_accessor :order
    15	
    16	    # Create the OrderMerger
    17	    #
    18	    # @api public
    19	    # @param [Spree::Order] order The order which line items will be merged
    20	    # into.
    21	    def initialize(order)
    22	      @order = order
    23	    end
    24	
    25	    # Merge a second order in to the order the OrderMerger was initialized with
    26	    #
    27	    # The line items from `other_order` will be merged in to the `order` for
    28	    # this OrderMerger object. If the line items are for the same variant, it
    29	    # will add the quantity of the incoming line item to the existing line item.
    30	    # Otherwise, it will assign the line item to the new order.
    31	    #
    32	    # After the orders have been merged the `other_order` will be destroyed.
    33	    #
    34	    # @example
    35	    #   initial_order = Spree::Order.find(1)
    36	    #   order_to_merge = Spree::Order.find(2)
    37	    #   merger = Spree::OrderMerger.new(initial_order)
    38	    #   merger.merge!(order_to_merge)
    39	    #   # order_to_merge is destroyed, initial order now contains the line items
    40	    #   # of order_to_merge
    41	    #
    42	    # @api public
    43	    # @param [Spree::Order] other_order An order which will be merged in to the
    44	    # order the OrderMerger was initialized with.
    45	    # @param [Spree::User] user Associate the order the user specified. If not
    46	    # specified, the order user association will not be changed.
    47	    # @return [void]
    48	    def merge!(other_order, user = nil)
    49	      if other_order.currency == order.currency
    50	        other_order.line_items.each do |other_order_line_item|
    51	          current_line_item = find_matching_line_item(other_order_line_item)
    52	          handle_merge(current_line_item, other_order_line_item)
    53	        end
    54	      end
    55	
    56	      set_user(user)
    57	      if order.valid?
    58	        persist_merge
    59	
    60	        # So that the destroy doesn't take out line items which may have been re-assigned
    61	        other_order.line_items.reload
    62	        other_order.destroy
    63	      end
    64	    end
    65	
    66	    private
    67	
    68	    # Retreive a matching line item from the existing order
    69	    #
    70	    # It will compare line items based on variants, and all line item
    71	    # comparison hooks on the order.
    72	    #
    73	    # @api private
    74	    # @param [Spree::LineItem] other_order_line_item The line item from
    75	    # `other_order` we are attempting to merge in.
    76	    # @return [Spree::LineItem] A matching line item from the order. nil if none
    77	    # exist.
    78	    def find_matching_line_item(other_order_line_item)
    79	      order.line_items.detect do |my_li|
    80	        my_li.variant == other_order_line_item.variant &&
    81	          Spree::Config.line_item_comparison_hooks.all? do |hook|
    82	            order.send(hook, my_li, other_order_line_item.serializable_hash)
    83	          end
    84	      end
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    # Associate the user with the order
    88	    #
    89	    # @api private
    90	    # @param [Spree::User] user The user to associate with the order. If nil
    91	    # the order user association will remain the same. If the order is already
    92	    # associated with a user, it will not be changed.
    93	    # @return [void]
    94	    def set_user(user)
    95	      order.associate_user!(user) if !order.user && user
    96	    end
    97	
    98	    # Merge the `other_order_line_item` into `current_line_item`
    99	    #
   100	    # If `current_line_item` is nil, the `other_order_line_item` will be
   101	    # re-assigned to the `order`.
   102	    #
   103	    # If the merged line item can not be saved, an error will be added to
   104	    # `order`.
   105	    #
   106	    # @api private
   107	    # @param [Spree::LineItem] current_line_item The line item to be merged
   108	    # into. If nil, the `other_order_line_item` will be re-assigned.
   109	    # @param [Spree::LineItem] other_order_line_item The line item to merge in.
   110	    # @return [void]
   111	    def handle_merge(current_line_item, other_order_line_item)
   112	      if current_line_item
   113	        current_line_item.quantity += other_order_line_item.quantity
   114	        handle_error(current_line_item) unless current_line_item.save
   115	      else
   116	        new_line_item = order.line_items.build(other_order_line_item.attributes.except("id"))
   117	        handle_error(new_line_item) unless new_line_item.save
   118	      end
   119	    end
   120	
   121	    # Handle an error from saving the `line_item`
   122	    #
   123	    # This adds errors from the line item to the `errors[:base]` of the order.
   124	    #
   125	    # @api private
   126	    # @param [Spree::LineItem] line_item The line item which could not be saved
   127	    # @return [void]
   128	    def handle_error(line_item)
   129	      order.errors.add(:base, line_item.errors.full_messages)
   130	    end
   131	
   132	    # Save the order totals after merge
   133	    #
   134	    # It triggers the order recalculator to ensure that item counts and totals are
   135	    # up to date.
   136	    #
   137	    # @api private
   138	    # @return [void]
   139	    def persist_merge
   140	      order.recalculate
   141	    end
   142	  end
   143	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  class OrderUpdater
     5	    attr_reader :order
     6	    delegate :payments, :line_items, :adjustments, :all_adjustments, :shipments, :quantity, to: :order
     7	
     8	    def initialize(order)
     9	      @order = order
    10	    end
    11	
    12	    # This is a multi-purpose method for processing logic related to changes in the Order.
    13	    # It is meant to be called from various observers so that the Order is aware of changes
    14	    # that affect totals and other values stored in the Order.
    15	    #
    16	    # This method should never do anything to the Order that results in a save call on the
    17	    # object with callbacks (otherwise you will end up in an infinite recursion as the
    18	    # associations try to save and then in turn try to call +update!+ again.)
    19	    def recalculate
    20	      order.transaction do
    21	        recalculate_line_item_prices
    22	        recalculate_item_count
    23	        update_shipment_amounts
    24	        update_totals
    25	        if order.completed?
    26	          recalculate_payment_state
    27	          update_shipments
    28	          recalculate_shipment_state
    29	        end
    30	        persist_totals
    31	      end
    32	      Spree::Bus.publish(:order_recalculated, order:)
    33	    end
    34	    alias_method :update, :recalculate
    35	    deprecate update: :recalculate, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
    36	
    37	    # Updates the +shipment_state+ attribute according to the following logic:
    38	    #
    39	    # shipped   when all Shipments are in the "shipped" state
    40	    # partial   when at least one Shipment has a state of "shipped" and there is another Shipment with a state other than "shipped"
    41	    #           or there are InventoryUnits associated with the order that have a state of "sold" but are not associated with a Shipment.
    42	    # ready     when all Shipments are in the "ready" state
    43	    # backorder when there is backordered inventory associated with an order
    44	    # pending   when all Shipments are in the "pending" state
    45	    #
    46	    # The +shipment_state+ value helps with reporting, etc. since it provides a quick and easy way to locate Orders needing attention.
    47	    def recalculate_shipment_state
    48	      log_state_change("shipment") do
    49	        order.shipment_state = determine_shipment_state
    50	      end
    51	
    52	      order.shipment_state
    53	    end
    54	    alias_method :update_shipment_state, :recalculate_shipment_state
    55	    deprecate update_shipment_state: :recalculate_shipment_state, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
    56	
    57	    # Recalculates the +payment_state+ attribute according to the following logic:
    58	    #
    59	    # paid          when +payment_total+ is equal to +total+
    60	    # balance_due   when +payment_total+ is less than +total+
    61	    # credit_owed   when +payment_total+ is greater than +total+
    62	    # failed        when most recent payment is in the failed state
    63	    # void          when the order has been canceled and the payment total is 0
    64	    #
    65	    # The +payment_state+ value helps with reporting, etc. since it provides a quick and easy way to locate Orders needing attention.
    66	    def recalculate_payment_state
    67	      log_state_change("payment") do
    68	        order.payment_state = determine_payment_state
    69	      end
    70	
    71	      order.payment_state
    72	    end
    73	    alias_method :update_payment_state, :recalculate_payment_state
    74	    deprecate update_payment_state: :recalculate_payment_state, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
    75	
    76	    private
    77	
    78	    def determine_payment_state
    79	      if payments.present? && payments.valid.empty? && order.outstanding_balance != 0
    80	        "failed"
    81	      elsif order.state == "canceled" && order.payment_total.zero?
    82	        "void"
    83	      elsif order.outstanding_balance > 0
    84	        "balance_due"
    85	      elsif order.outstanding_balance < 0
    86	        "credit_owed"
    87	      else
    88	        # outstanding_balance == 0
    89	        "paid"
    90	      end
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    def determine_shipment_state
    94	      if order.backordered?
    95	        "backorder"
    96	      else
    97	        # get all the shipment states for this order
    98	        shipment_states = shipments.states
    99	        if shipment_states.size > 1
   100	          # multiple shiment states means it's most likely partially shipped
   101	          "partial"
   102	        else
   103	          # will return nil if no shipments are found
   104	          shipment_states.first
   105	        end
   106	      end
   107	    end
   108	
   109	    # This will update and select the best promotion adjustment, update tax
   110	    # adjustments, update cancellation adjustments, and then update the total
   111	    # fields (promo_total, included_tax_total, additional_tax_total, and
   112	    # adjustment_total) on the item.
   113	    # @return [void]
   114	    def update_adjustments
   115	      # Promotion adjustments must be applied first, then tax adjustments.
   116	      # This fits the criteria for VAT tax as outlined here:
   117	      # http://www.hmrc.gov.uk/vat/managing/charging/discounts-etc.htm#1
   118	      # It also fits the criteria for sales tax as outlined here:
   119	      # http://www.boe.ca.gov/formspubs/pub113/
   120	      update_promotions
   121	      update_tax_adjustments
   122	      recalculate_item_totals
   123	    end
   124	    alias_method :recalculate_adjustments, :update_adjustments
   125	    deprecate recalculate_adjustments: :update_adjustments, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   126	
   127	    # Updates the following Order total values:
   128	    #
   129	    # +payment_total+      The total value of all finalized Payments (NOTE: non-finalized Payments are excluded)
   130	    # +item_total+         The total value of all LineItems
   131	    # +adjustment_total+   The total value of all adjustments (promotions, credits, etc.)
   132	    # +promo_total+        The total value of all promotion adjustments
   133	    # +total+              The so-called "order total."  This is equivalent to +item_total+ plus +adjustment_total+.
   134	    def update_totals
   135	      recalculate_payment_total
   136	      recalculate_item_total
   137	      recalculate_shipment_total
   138	      update_adjustment_total
   139	    end
   140	
   141	    def update_shipment_amounts
   142	      shipments.each(&:update_amounts)
   143	    end
   144	
   145	    # give each of the shipments a chance to update themselves
   146	    def update_shipments
   147	      shipments.each(&:update_state)
   148	    end
   149	
   150	    def recalculate_payment_total
   151	      order.payment_total = payments.completed.includes(:refunds).sum { |payment| payment.amount - payment.refunds.sum(:amount) }
   152	    end
   153	    alias_method :update_payment_total, :recalculate_payment_total
   154	    deprecate update_payment_total: :recalculate_payment_total, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   155	
   156	    def recalculate_shipment_total
   157	      order.shipment_total = shipments.to_a.sum(&:cost)
   158	      recalculate_order_total
   159	    end
   160	    alias_method :update_shipment_total, :recalculate_shipment_total
   161	    deprecate update_shipment_total: :recalculate_shipment_total, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   162	
   163	    def recalculate_order_total
   164	      order.total = order.item_total + order.shipment_total + order.adjustment_total
   165	    end
   166	    alias_method :update_order_total, :recalculate_order_total
   167	    deprecate update_order_total: :recalculate_order_total, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   168	
   169	    def update_adjustment_total
   170	      update_adjustments
   171	
   172	      all_items = (line_items + shipments).reject(&:marked_for_destruction?)
   173	      # Ignore any adjustments that have been marked for destruction in our
   174	      # calculations. They'll get removed when/if we persist the order.
   175	      valid_adjustments = adjustments.reject(&:marked_for_destruction?)
   176	      order_tax_adjustments = valid_adjustments.select(&:tax?)
   177	
   178	      order.adjustment_total = all_items.sum(&:adjustment_total) + valid_adjustments.sum(&:amount)
   179	      order.included_tax_total = all_items.sum(&:included_tax_total) + order_tax_adjustments.select(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
   180	      order.additional_tax_total = all_items.sum(&:additional_tax_total) + order_tax_adjustments.reject(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
   181	
   182	      recalculate_order_total
   183	    end
   184	
   185	    def recalculate_item_count
   186	      order.item_count = line_items.to_a.sum(&:quantity)
   187	    end
   188	    alias_method :update_item_count, :recalculate_item_count
   189	    deprecate update_item_count: :recalculate_item_count, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   190	
   191	    def recalculate_item_total
   192	      order.item_total = line_items.to_a.sum(&:amount)
   193	      recalculate_order_total
   194	    end
   195	    alias_method :update_item_total, :recalculate_item_total
   196	    deprecate update_item_total: :recalculate_item_total, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   197	
   198	    def persist_totals
   199	      order.save!
   200	    end
   201	
   202	    def log_state_change(name)
   203	      state = "#{name}_state"
   204	      old_state = order.public_send(state)
   205	      yield
   206	      new_state = order.public_send(state)
   207	      if old_state != new_state
   208	        StateChangeTrackingJob.perform_later(
   209	          order,
   210	          old_state,
   211	          new_state,
   212	          Time.current,
   213	          name
   214	        )
   215	      end
   216	    end
   217	
   218	    def update_promotions
   219	      Spree::Config.promotions.order_adjuster_class.new(order).call
   220	    end
   221	
   222	    def update_tax_adjustments
   223	      Spree::Config.tax_adjuster_class.new(order).adjust!
   224	    end
   225	
   226	    def update_cancellations
   227	    end
   228	    deprecate :update_cancellations, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   229	
   230	    def recalculate_item_totals
   231	      [*line_items, *shipments].each do |item|
   232	        Spree::Config.item_total_class.new(item).recalculate!
   233	
   234	        next unless item.changed?
   235	
   236	        item.assign_attributes(
   237	          promo_total: item.promo_total,
   238	          included_tax_total: item.included_tax_total,
   239	          additional_tax_total: item.additional_tax_total,
   240	          adjustment_total: item.adjustment_total
   241	        )
   242	      end
   243	    end
   244	
   245	    def recalculate_line_item_prices
   246	      if Spree::Config.recalculate_cart_prices
   247	        line_items.each(&:recalculate_price)
   248	      end
   249	    end
   250	  end
   251	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	require "spree/manipulative_query_monitor"
     4	
     5	module Spree
     6	  class InMemoryOrderUpdater
     7	    attr_reader :order
     8	
     9	    # logs a warning when a manipulative query is made when the persist flag is set to false
    10	    class_attribute :log_manipulative_queries
    11	    self.log_manipulative_queries = true
    12	
    13	    delegate :payments, :line_items, :adjustments, :all_adjustments, :shipments, :quantity, to: :order
    14	
    15	    def initialize(order)
    16	      @order = order
    17	    end
    18	
    19	    # This is a multi-purpose method for processing logic related to changes in the Order.
    20	    # It is meant to be called from various observers so that the Order is aware of changes
    21	    # that affect totals and other values stored in the Order.
    22	    #
    23	    # This method should never do anything to the Order that results in a save call on the
    24	    # object with callbacks (otherwise you will end up in an infinite recursion as the
    25	    # associations try to save and then in turn try to call +update!+ again.)
    26	    def recalculate(persist: true)
    27	      monitor =
    28	        if log_manipulative_queries
    29	          Spree::ManipulativeQueryMonitor
    30	        else
    31	          proc { |&block| block.call }
    32	        end
    33	
    34	      order.transaction do
    35	        monitor.call do
    36	          recalculate_line_item_prices
    37	          recalculate_item_count
    38	          assign_shipment_amounts
    39	        end
    40	
    41	        if persist
    42	          update_totals(persist:)
    43	        else
    44	          monitor.call do
    45	            update_totals(persist:)
    46	          end
    47	        end
    48	
    49	        monitor.call do
    50	          if order.completed?
    51	            recalculate_payment_state
    52	            recalculate_shipment_state
    53	          end
    54	        end
    55	
    56	        persist_totals if persist
    57	      end
    58	
    59	      Spree::Bus.publish(:order_recalculated, order:) if persist
    60	    end
    61	    alias_method :update, :recalculate
    62	    deprecate update: :recalculate, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
    63	
    64	    # Recalculates the state on all of them shipments, then recalculates the
    65	    # +shipment_state+ attribute according to the following logic:
    66	    #
    67	    # shipped   when all Shipments are in the "shipped" state
    68	    # partial   when at least one Shipment has a state of "shipped" and there is another Shipment with a state other than "shipped"
    69	    #           or there are InventoryUnits associated with the order that have a state of "sold" but are not associated with a Shipment.
    70	    # ready     when all Shipments are in the "ready" state
    71	    # backorder when there is backordered inventory associated with an order
    72	    # pending   when all Shipments are in the "pending" state
    73	    #
    74	    # The +shipment_state+ value helps with reporting, etc. since it provides a quick and easy way to locate Orders needing attention.
    75	    def recalculate_shipment_state
    76	      shipments.each(&:recalculate_state)
    77	
    78	      order.shipment_state = determine_shipment_state
    79	      order.shipment_state
    80	    end
    81	    alias_method :update_shipment_state, :recalculate_shipment_state
    82	    deprecate update_shipment_state: :recalculate_shipment_state, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
    83	
    84	    # Recalculates the +payment_state+ attribute according to the following logic:
    85	    #
    86	    # paid          when +payment_total+ is equal to +total+
    87	    # balance_due   when +payment_total+ is less than +total+
    88	    # credit_owed   when +payment_total+ is greater than +total+
    89	    # failed        when most recent payment is in the failed state
    90	    # void          when the order has been canceled and the payment total is 0
    91	    #
    92	    # The +payment_state+ value helps with reporting, etc. since it provides a quick and easy way to locate Orders needing attention.
    93	    def recalculate_payment_state
    94	      order.payment_state = determine_payment_state
    95	      order.payment_state
    96	    end
    97	    alias_method :update_payment_state, :recalculate_payment_state
    98	    deprecate update_payment_state: :recalculate_payment_state, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
    99	
   100	    private
   101	
   102	    def determine_payment_state
   103	      if payments.present? && payments.valid.empty? && order.outstanding_balance != 0
   104	        "failed"
   105	      elsif order.state == "canceled" && order.payment_total.zero?
   106	        "void"
   107	      elsif order.outstanding_balance > 0
   108	        "balance_due"
   109	      elsif order.outstanding_balance < 0
   110	        "credit_owed"
   111	      else
   112	        # outstanding_balance == 0
   113	        "paid"
   114	      end
   115	    end
   116	
   117	    def determine_shipment_state
   118	      if order.backordered?
   119	        "backorder"
   120	      else
   121	        # get all the shipment states for this order
   122	        shipment_states = shipments.states
   123	        if shipment_states.size > 1
   124	          # multiple shiment states means it's most likely partially shipped
   125	          "partial"
   126	        else
   127	          # will return nil if no shipments are found
   128	          shipment_states.first
   129	        end
   130	      end
   131	    end
   132	
   133	    # This will update and select the best promotion adjustment, update tax
   134	    # adjustments, update cancellation adjustments, and then update the total
   135	    # fields (promo_total, included_tax_total, additional_tax_total, and
   136	    # adjustment_total) on the item.
   137	    # @return [void]
   138	    def update_adjustments(persist:)
   139	      # Promotion adjustments must be applied first, then tax adjustments.
   140	      # This fits the criteria for VAT tax as outlined here:
   141	      # http://www.hmrc.gov.uk/vat/managing/charging/discounts-etc.htm#1
   142	      # It also fits the criteria for sales tax as outlined here:
   143	      # http://www.boe.ca.gov/formspubs/pub113/
   144	      update_promotions(persist:)
   145	      update_tax_adjustments
   146	      assign_item_totals
   147	    end
   148	
   149	    # Updates the following Order total values:
   150	    #
   151	    # +payment_total+      The total value of all finalized Payments (NOTE: non-finalized Payments are excluded)
   152	    # +item_total+         The total value of all LineItems
   153	    # +adjustment_total+   The total value of all adjustments (promotions, credits, etc.)
   154	    # +promo_total+        The total value of all promotion adjustments
   155	    # +total+              The so-called "order total."  This is equivalent to +item_total+ plus +adjustment_total+.
   156	    def update_totals(persist:)
   157	      recalculate_payment_total
   158	      recalculate_item_total
   159	      recalculate_shipment_total
   160	      update_adjustment_total(persist:)
   161	    end

   163	    def assign_shipment_amounts

   165	    end

   167	    def update_adjustment_total(persist:)
   168	      update_adjustments(persist:)
   169	
   170	      all_items = line_items + shipments
   171	      # Ignore any adjustments that have been marked for destruction in our
   172	      # calculations. They'll get removed when/if we persist the order.
   173	      valid_adjustments = adjustments.reject(&:marked_for_destruction?)
   174	      order_tax_adjustments = valid_adjustments.select(&:tax?)
   175	
   176	      order.adjustment_total = all_items.sum(&:adjustment_total) + valid_adjustments.sum(&:amount)
   177	      order.included_tax_total = all_items.sum(&:included_tax_total) + order_tax_adjustments.select(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
   178	      order.additional_tax_total = all_items.sum(&:additional_tax_total) + order_tax_adjustments.reject(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
   179	
   180	      recalculate_order_total
   181	    end

   183	    def update_promotions(persist:)

   185	    end

   187	    def update_tax_adjustments

   189	    end
   190	
   191	    def update_cancellations
   192	    end
   193	    deprecate :update_cancellations, deprecator: Spree.deprecator

   195	    def assign_item_totals
   196	      [*line_items, *shipments].each do |item|
   197	        Spree::Config.item_total_class.new(item).recalculate!
   198	
   199	        next unless item.changed?
   200	
   201	        item.assign_attributes(
   202	          promo_total: item.promo_total,
   203	          included_tax_total: item.included_tax_total,
   204	          additional_tax_total: item.additional_tax_total,
   205	          adjustment_total: item.adjustment_total
   206	        )
   207	      end
   208	    end

   210	    def recalculate_payment_total
   211	      order.payment_total = payments.completed.includes(:refunds).sum { |payment| payment.amount - payment.refunds.sum(:amount) }
   212	    end
   213	
   214	    def recalculate_shipment_total
   215	      order.shipment_total = shipments.to_a.sum(&:cost)
   216	      recalculate_order_total
   217	    end
   218	
   219	    def recalculate_order_total
   220	      order.total = order.item_total + order.shipment_total + order.adjustment_total
   221	    end
   222

   225	    end

   228	      order.item_total = line_items.to_a.sum(&:amount)
   229	      recalculate_order_total
   230	    end

   234	        line_items.each(&:recalculate_price)
   235	      end
   236	    end

   240	      log_state_change("shipment")

   242	    end

   244	    def log_state_change(name)
   245	      state = "#{name}_state"
   246	      previous_state, current_state = order.changes[state]
   247	
   248	      if previous_state != current_state
   249	        # Enqueue the job to track this state change
   250	        StateChangeTrackingJob.perform_later(
   251	          order,
   252	          previous_state,
   253	          current_state,
   254	          Time.current,
   255	          name
   256	        )
   257	      end
   258	    end
   259	  end

The central order contract is not just `core/app/models/spree/order.rb`; it delegates to configurable classes for contents, shipping, cancellations, recalculation, stock coordination, promotion adjustment, and tax adjustment. I’m expanding the audit around those extension points because they are the places a teardown/recalculation change is most likely to leak.

## core/app/models/spree/fulfilment_changer.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  # Service class to change fulfilment of inventory units of a particular variant
     5	  # to another shipment. The other shipment would typically have a different
     6	  # shipping method, stock location or delivery date, such that we actually change
     7	  # the planned fulfilment for the items in question.
     8	  #
     9	  # Can be used to merge shipments by moving all items to another shipment, because
    10	  # this class will delete any empty original shipment.
    11	  #
    12	  # @attr [Spree::Shipment] current_shipment The shipment we transfer units from
    13	  # @attr [Spree::Shipment] desired_shipment The shipment we want to move units onto
    14	  # @attr [Spree::StockLocation] current_stock_location The stock location of the current shipment
    15	  # @attr [Spree::StockLocation] desired_stock_location The stock location of the desired shipment
    16	  # @attr [Spree::Variant] variant We only move units that represent this variant
    17	  # @attr [Integer] quantity How many units we want to move
    18	  #
    19	  class FulfilmentChanger
    20	    include ActiveModel::Validations
    21	
    22	    attr_accessor :current_shipment, :desired_shipment
    23	    attr_reader :variant, :quantity, :current_stock_location, :desired_stock_location, :track_inventory
    24	
    25	    def initialize(current_shipment:, desired_shipment:, variant:, quantity:, track_inventory:)
    26	      @current_shipment = current_shipment
    27	      @desired_shipment = desired_shipment
    28	      @current_stock_location = current_shipment.stock_location
    29	      @desired_stock_location = desired_shipment.stock_location
    30	      @variant = variant
    31	      @quantity = quantity
    32	      @track_inventory = track_inventory
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    validates :quantity, numericality: {greater_than: 0}
    36	    validate :current_shipment_not_already_shipped
    37	    validate :desired_shipment_different_from_current
    38	    validates :desired_stock_location, presence: true
    39	    validate :enough_stock_at_desired_location, if: :handle_stock_counts?
    40	
    41	    # Performs the change of fulfilment
    42	    # @return [true, false] Whether the requested fulfilment change was successful
    43	    def run!
    44	      # Validations here are intended to catch all necessary prerequisites.
    45	      # We return early so all checks have happened already.
    46	      return false if invalid?
    47	      desired_shipment.save! if desired_shipment.new_record?
    48	
    49	      if track_inventory
    50	        run_tracking_inventory
    51	      else
    52	        run_without_tracking_inventory
    53	      end
    54	
    55	      # We modified the inventory units at the database level for speed reasons.
    56	      # The downside of that is that we need to reload the associations.
    57	      current_shipment.inventory_units.reload
    58	      desired_shipment.inventory_units.reload
    59	
    60	      # If the current shipment now has no inventory units left, we won't need it any longer.
    61	      if current_shipment.inventory_units.length.zero?
    62	        current_shipment.destroy!
    63	      else
    64	        # The current shipment has changed, so we need to make sure that shipping rates
    65	        # have the correct amount.
    66	        current_shipment.refresh_rates
    67	      end
    68	
    69	      # The desired shipment has also change, so we need to make sure shipping rates
    70	      # are up-to-date, too.
    71	      desired_shipment.refresh_rates
    72	
    73	      # In order to reflect the changes in the order totals
    74	      desired_shipment.order.reload
    75	      desired_shipment.order.recalculate
    76	
    77	      true
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    private
    81	
    82	    # When moving things from one stock location to another, we need to restock items
    83	    # from the current location and unstock them at the desired location.
    84	    # Also, when we want to track inventory changes, we need to make sure that we have
    85	    # enough stock at the desired location to fulfil the order. Based on how many items
    86	    # we can take from the desired location, we could end up with some items being backordered.
    87	    def run_tracking_inventory
    88	      # Retrieve how many on hand items we can take from desired stock location
    89	      available_quantity = get_available_quantity
    90	      new_on_hand_quantity = [available_quantity, quantity].min
    91	      backordered_quantity = get_backordered_quantity(available_quantity, new_on_hand_quantity)
    92	
    93	      # Determine how many backordered and on_hand items we'll need to move. We
    94	      # don't want to move more than what's being asked. And we can't move a
    95	      # negative amount, which is why we need to perform our min/max logic here.
    96	      backordered_quantity_to_move = [backordered_quantity, quantity].min
    97	      on_hand_quantity_to_move = [quantity - backordered_quantity_to_move, 0].max
    98	
    99	      ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
   100	        if handle_stock_counts?
   101	          # We only run this query if we need it.
   102	          current_on_hand_quantity = [current_shipment.inventory_units.pre_shipment.size, quantity].min
   103	          unstock_quantity = desired_shipment.stock_location.backorderable?(variant) ? quantity : new_on_hand_quantity
   104	
   105	          # Restock things we will not fulfil from the current shipment anymore
   106	          current_stock_location.restock(variant, current_on_hand_quantity, current_shipment)
   107	          # Unstock what we will fulfil with the new shipment
   108	          desired_stock_location.unstock(variant, unstock_quantity, desired_shipment)
   109	        end
   110	
   111	        # These two statements are the heart of this class. We change the number
   112	        # of inventory units requested from one shipment to the other.
   113	        # We order by state, because `'backordered' < 'on_hand'`.
   114	        # We start to move the new actual backordered quantity, so the remaining
   115	        # quantity can be set to on_hand state.
   116	        current_shipment
   117	          .inventory_units
   118	          .where(variant:)
   119	          .order(state: :asc)
   120	          .limit(backordered_quantity_to_move)
   121	          .update_all(shipment_id: desired_shipment.id, state: :backordered)
   122	
   123	        current_shipment
   124	          .inventory_units
   125	          .where(variant:)
   126	          .order(state: :asc)
   127	          .limit(on_hand_quantity_to_move)
   128	          .update_all(shipment_id: desired_shipment.id, state: :on_hand)
   129	      end
   130	    end
   131	
   132	    # When we don't track inventory, we can just move the inventory units from one shipment
   133	    # to the other.
   134	    def run_without_tracking_inventory
   135	      ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
   136	        current_shipment
   137	          .inventory_units
   138	          .where(variant:)
   139	          .order(state: :asc)
   140	          .limit(quantity)
   141	          .update_all(shipment_id: desired_shipment.id)
   142	      end
   143	    end
   144	
   145	    # We don't need to handle stock counts for incomplete orders. Also, if
   146	    # the new shipment and the desired shipment will ship from the same stock location,
   147	    # unstocking and restocking will not be necessary.
   148	    def handle_stock_counts?
   149	      current_shipment.order.completed? && current_stock_location != desired_stock_location
   150	    end
   151	
   152	    def get_available_quantity
   153	      if current_stock_location != desired_stock_location
   154	        desired_location_quantifier.positive_stock
   155	      else
   156	        sl_availability = current_location_quantifier.positive_stock
   157	        shipment_availability = current_shipment.inventory_units.where(variant: variant).on_hand.count
   158	        sl_availability + shipment_availability
   159	      end
   160	    end
   161	
   162	    def get_backordered_quantity(available_quantity, new_on_hand_quantity)
   163	      if current_stock_location != desired_stock_location
   164	        quantity - new_on_hand_quantity
   165	      else
   166	        shipment_quantity = current_shipment.inventory_units.where(variant: variant).size
   167	        shipment_quantity - available_quantity
   168	      end
   169	    end
   170	
   171	    def current_shipment_not_already_shipped
   172	      if current_shipment.shipped?
   173	        errors.add(:current_shipment, :has_already_been_shipped)
   174	      end
   175	    end
   176	
   177	    def enough_stock_at_desired_location
   178	      unless desired_location_quantifier.can_supply?(quantity)
   179	        errors.add(:desired_shipment, :not_enough_stock_at_desired_location)
   180	      end
   181	    end
   182	
   183	    def desired_location_quantifier
   184	      @desired_location_quantifier ||= Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant, desired_stock_location)
   185	    end
   186	
   187	    def current_location_quantifier
   188	      @current_location_quantifier ||= Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant, current_stock_location)
   189	    end
   190	
   191	    def desired_shipment_different_from_current
   192	      if desired_shipment.id == current_shipment.id
   193	        errors.add(:desired_shipment, :can_not_transfer_within_same_shipment)
   194	      end
   195	    end
   196	  end
   197	end

## core/app/models/spree/exchange.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  class Exchange
     5	    class UnableToCreateShipments < StandardError; end
     6	    extend ActiveModel::Naming
     7	
     8	    def initialize(order, reimbursement_objects)
     9	      @order = order
    10	      @reimbursement_objects = reimbursement_objects
    11	    end
    12	
    13	    def description
    14	      @reimbursement_objects.map do |reimbursement_object|
    15	        "#{reimbursement_object.variant.options_text} => #{reimbursement_object.exchange_variant.options_text}"
    16	      end.join(" | ")
    17	    end
    18	
    19	    def display_amount
    20	      Spree::Money.new @reimbursement_objects.sum(&:total)
    21	    end
    22	
    23	    def perform!
    24	      begin
    25	        shipments = Spree::Config.stock.coordinator_class.new(
    26	          @order,
    27	          inventory_units: @reimbursement_objects.map(&:build_exchange_inventory_unit)
    28	        ).shipments
    29	      rescue Spree::Order::InsufficientStock
    30	        raise UnableToCreateShipments.new("Could not generate shipments for all items. Out of stock?")
    31	      end
    32	      @order.shipments += shipments
    33	      @order.save!
    34	      shipments.each do |shipment|
    35	        shipment.update_state
    36	        shipment.finalize!
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    def to_key
    41	      nil
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    def self.param_key
    45	      "spree_exchange"
    46	    end
    47	  end
    48	end

## core/app/models/spree/unit_cancel.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	# This represents an inventory unit that has been canceled from an order after it has already been completed
     4	# The reason specifies why it was canceled.
     5	# This class should encapsulate logic related to canceling inventory after order complete
     6	class Spree::UnitCancel < Spree::Base
     7	  SHORT_SHIP = "Short Ship"
     8	  DEFAULT_REASON = "Cancel"
     9	
    10	  belongs_to :inventory_unit, optional: true
    11	  has_one :adjustment, as: :source, dependent: :destroy
    12	
    13	  validates :inventory_unit, presence: true
    14	
    15	  # Creates necessary cancel adjustments for the line item.
    16	  def adjust!
    17	    raise "Adjustment is already created" if adjustment
    18	
    19	    amount = compute_amount(inventory_unit.line_item)
    20	
    21	    self.adjustment = inventory_unit.line_item.adjustments.create!(
    22	      source: self,
    23	      amount:,
    24	      order: inventory_unit.order,
    25	      label: "#{I18n.t("spree.cancellation")} - #{reason}",
    26	      finalized: true
    27	    )
    28	
    29	    inventory_unit.line_item.order.recalculate
    30	    adjustment
    31	  end
    32	
    33	  # This method is used by Adjustment#update to recalculate the cost.
    34	  def compute_amount(line_item)
    35	    raise "Adjustable does not match line item" unless line_item == inventory_unit.line_item
    36	
    37	    -weighted_line_item_amount(line_item)
    38	  end
    39	
    40	  private
    41	
    42	  def weighted_line_item_amount(line_item)
    43	    quantity_of_line_item = quantity_of_line_item(line_item)
    44	    raise ZeroDivisionError, "Line Item does not have any inventory units available to cancel" if quantity_of_line_item.zero?
    45	
    46	    line_item.total_before_tax / quantity_of_line_item
    47	  end
    48	
    49	  def quantity_of_line_item(line_item)
    50	    BigDecimal(line_item.inventory_units.not_canceled.count { |element| !element.original_return_item })
    51	  end
    52	end

## core/app/models/spree/shipping_manifest.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class Spree::ShippingManifest
     4	  ManifestItem = Struct.new(:line_item, :variant, :quantity, :states)
     5	
     6	  def initialize(inventory_units:)
     7	    @inventory_units = inventory_units.to_a
     8	  end
     9	
    10	  def for_order(order)
    11	    Spree::ShippingManifest.new(
    12	      inventory_units: @inventory_units.select { |iu| iu.order_id == order.id }
    13	    )
    14	  end
    15	
    16	  def items
    17	    # Grouping by the ID means that we don't have to call out to the association accessor
    18	    # This makes the grouping by faster because it results in less SQL cache hits.
    19	    @inventory_units.group_by(&:variant_id).flat_map do |_variant_id, variant_units|
    20	      variant_units.group_by(&:line_item_id).map do |_line_item_id, units|
    21	        states = {}
    22	        units.group_by(&:state).each { |state, iu| states[state] = iu.count }
    23	
    24	        first_unit = units.first
    25	
    26	        ManifestItem.new(first_unit.line_item, first_unit.variant, units.length, states)
    27	      end
    28	    end
    29	  end
    30	end

## core/app/models/spree/stock/simple_coordinator.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  module Stock
     5	    # A simple implementation of Stock Coordination
     6	    #
     7	    # The algorithm for allocating inventory is naive:
     8	    #   * For each available Stock Location
     9	    #     * Allocate as much on hand inventory as possible from this location
    10	    #     * Remove the amount allocated from the amount desired
    11	    #   * Repeat but for backordered inventory
    12	    #   * Combine allocated and on hand inventory into a single shipment per-location
    13	    #
    14	    # Allocation logic can be changed using a custom class (as
    15	    # configured in Spree::Config::stock_allocator_class )
    16	    #
    17	    # After allocation, splitters are run on each Package (as configured in
    18	    # Spree::Config.environment.stock_splitters)
    19	    #
    20	    # Finally, shipping rates are calculated using the class configured as
    21	    # Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.
    22	    class SimpleCoordinator
    23	      attr_reader :order
    24	
    25	      # @api private
    26	      attr_reader :inventory_units, :splitters, :stock_locations,
    27	        :filtered_stock_locations, :inventory_units_by_variant, :desired,
    28	        :availability, :allocator, :packages
    29	
    30	      def initialize(order, inventory_units_deprecated = nil, inventory_units: nil)
    31	        if inventory_units_deprecated
    32	          Spree.deprecator.warn "Using the `inventory_units` positional " \
    33	            "argument is deprecated in favor of using the keyword argument. "
    34	
    35	          inventory_units ||= inventory_units_deprecated
    36	        end
    37	
    38	        @order = order
    39	        @inventory_units =
    40	          inventory_units || Spree::Config.stock.inventory_unit_builder_class.new(order).units
    41	        @splitters = Spree::Config.environment.stock_splitters
    42	
    43	        @filtered_stock_locations = Spree::Config.stock.location_filter_class.new(load_stock_locations, order).filter
    44	        sorted_stock_locations = Spree::Config.stock.location_sorter_class.new(filtered_stock_locations).sort
    45	        @stock_locations = sorted_stock_locations
    46	
    47	        @inventory_units_by_variant = @inventory_units.group_by(&:variant)
    48	        @desired = Spree::StockQuantities.new(inventory_units_by_variant.transform_values(&:count))
    49	        @availability = Spree::Stock::Availability.new(
    50	          variants: desired.variants,
    51	          stock_locations:
    52	        )
    53	
    54	        @allocator = Spree::Config.stock.allocator_class.new(availability)
    55	      end
    56	
    57	      def shipments
    58	        @shipments ||= begin
    59	          @packages = build_packages
    60	          shipments = build_shipments
    61	
    62	          # Make sure we don't add the proposed shipments to the order
    63	          order.shipments = order.shipments - shipments
    64	
    65	          shipments
    66	        end
    67	      end
    68	
    69	      private
    70	
    71	      def load_stock_locations
    72	        Spree::StockLocation.all
    73	      end
    74	
    75	      def build_shipments
    76	        # Turn the Stock::Packages into a Shipment with rates
    77	        packages.map do |package|
    78	          shipment = package.shipment = package.to_shipment
    79	          shipment.shipping_rates = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new.shipping_rates(package)
    80	          shipment
    81	        end
    82	      end
    83	
    84	      def build_packages
    85	        # Allocate any available on hand inventory and remaining desired inventory from backorders
    86	        on_hand_packages, backordered_packages, leftover = allocator.allocate_inventory(desired)
    87	
    88	        raise Spree::Order::InsufficientStock.new(items: leftover.quantities) unless leftover.empty?
    89	
    90	        packages = stock_locations.map do |stock_location|
    91	          # Combine on_hand and backorders into a single package per-location
    92	          on_hand = on_hand_packages[stock_location.id] || Spree::StockQuantities.new
    93	          backordered = backordered_packages[stock_location.id] || Spree::StockQuantities.new
    94	
    95	          # Skip this location it has no inventory
    96	          next if on_hand.empty? && backordered.empty?
    97	
    98	          # Turn our raw quantities into a Stock::Package
    99	          package = Spree::Stock::Package.new(stock_location)
   100	          package.add_multiple(get_units(on_hand), :on_hand)
   101	          package.add_multiple(get_units(backordered), :backordered)
   102	
   103	          package
   104	        end.compact
   105	
   106	        # Split the packages
   107	        split_packages(packages)
   108	      end
   109	
   110	      def split_packages(initial_packages)
   111	        initial_packages.flat_map do |initial_package|
   112	          stock_location = initial_package.stock_location
   113	          Spree::Stock::SplitterChain.new(stock_location, @splitters).split([initial_package])
   114	        end
   115	      end
   116	
   117	      def get_units(quantities)
   118	        # Change our raw quantities back into inventory units
   119	        quantities.flat_map do |variant, quantity|
   120	          inventory_units_by_variant[variant].shift(quantity)
   121	        end
   122	      end
   123	    end
   124	  end
   125	end

## core/app/models/spree/stock/inventory_units_finalizer.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  module Stock
     5	    # Service class to finalize inventory units, it means unstock the desired
     6	    # quantity from related stock item and updates the given inventory units to
     7	    # not be pending.
     8	    class InventoryUnitsFinalizer
     9	      # @attr_reader [Spree::InventoryUnit] inventory_units to be finalized
    10	      attr_reader :inventory_units
    11	
    12	      # @param [Spree::InventoryUnit] inventory_units to be finalized
    13	      def initialize(inventory_units)
    14	        @inventory_units = inventory_units
    15	      end
    16	
    17	      # Finalize the inventory units, unstock and mark them as not pending.
    18	      def run!
    19	        Spree::InventoryUnit.transaction do
    20	          unstock_inventory_units
    21	          finalize_inventory_units
    22	        end
    23	      end
    24	
    25	      private
    26	
    27	      def finalize_inventory_units
    28	        inventory_units.map do |iu|
    29	          iu.update_columns(
    30	            pending: false,
    31	            updated_at: Time.current
    32	          )
    33	        end
    34	      end
    35	
    36	      def unstock_inventory_units
    37	        inventory_units.group_by(&:shipment).each do |shipment, units_by_shipment|
    38	          units_by_shipment.group_by(&:variant).each do |variant, units|
    39	            shipment.stock_location.unstock variant, units.count, shipment
    40	          end
    41	        end
    42	      end
    43	    end
    44	  end
    45	end

## core/app/models/spree/stock/availability_validator.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
